# Same Sky, Different Ground

> A free, practical guide to reading the ecology of the Sunshine Coast, Queensland, Australia — from the reef to the range. The companion resource to the natural-history book of the same name: what lives where, why the landscape is arranged the way it is, and how to read it for yourself.

The Sunshine Coast packs reef, estuary, heath, swamp, eucalypt forest and rainforest into a short, steep run from the sea to the range. This site explains why — the rock, soil, water and fire behind the pattern — and turns it into a skill anyone can use outdoors. All content is free to read and made to be useful on its own, not just as a promotion for the book.

Author: Daniel Addington. Language: en-AU. Region: Sunshine Coast (Maroochy, Mooloolah, Noosa and Mary catchments; Glass House Mountains to Cooloola; Blackall Range hinterland), Queensland, Australia.

## Guides

- [How to read the country — field guide](https://sunshinecoastecology.com.au/field-guide.html): The five-minute method for reading any patch of the coast at a glance; how to look, the one-day gradient drive, where to go, what's safe, and how to help.
- [The ecosystems](https://sunshinecoastecology.com.au/ecosystems.html): The six habitats from reef to range — estuaries and mangroves, dunes and wallum, paperbark swamps, eucalypt forests, rainforests, and sea country — how to recognise each, why it's there, who lives there, where to see it.
- [The forces](https://sunshinecoastecology.com.au/forces.html): Rock, soil, water and fire — the four forces that build every kind of country on the coast. Ecosystems are the what; these are the why.
- [Wonders](https://sunshinecoastecology.com.au/wonders.html): The set-piece natural wonders — dead volcanoes on the skyline (the Glass House Mountains), a giant podzol soil twenty metres deep at Cooloola, wallum frogs that breed in acid water, godwits that fly from the Arctic, and the humpback whales that came back from near-extinction.

## Living data

- [In flower now](https://sunshinecoastecology.com.au/in-flower.html): A living calendar of Sunshine Coast wildflowers by habitat and by month, from the spring wallum heath to the winter ironbarks.
- [This month](https://sunshinecoastecology.com.au/this-month.html): What to look for right now, through the naturalist's year — spring wildflowers to winter whales.
- [Explore the map](https://sunshinecoastecology.com.au/explore-map.html): An interactive relief map of the Sunshine Coast built from elevation data; click the landmarks to read what they are and why they are there.
- [Read the country — a game](https://sunshinecoastecology.com.au/read-the-country.html): Guess which ecosystem you are standing in from its cues alone.

## The book

- [The book](https://sunshinecoastecology.com.au/the-book.html): *Same Sky, Different Ground: reading the ecology of the Sunshine Coast, from the reef to the range* — a whole-picture natural history in nineteen chapters, available as an EPUB (ISBN 978-0-646-74477-3).
- [About](https://sunshinecoastecology.com.au/about.html): About the project, the author, and how it was made (including its use of AI tools).

## Notes

- The Sunshine Coast is Kabi Kabi (Gubbi Gubbi) Country. This site treats cultural material only from the published, public record and keeps its focus on ecology; it makes no claim to tell the Kabi Kabi people's cultural story.
- Scientific claims draw on published sources; where a figure or claim still needs checking against a primary source, the text says so.

## Citation

If you draw on this material, please attribute it to the book it accompanies:

> Addington, Daniel. *Same Sky, Different Ground: reading the ecology of the Sunshine Coast, from the reef to the range.* ISBN 978-0-646-74477-3. https://sunshinecoastecology.com.au/
